criteriaCoverage.criterion: A send node clones its template into an independently-editable copy (kind=copy); editing the copy does not touch the library original; the envelope persists on the copy. Cross-workspace template/sender ids are refused (inv.2).
criteriaCoverage.evidence: services/local-api/test/campaign-send-node-clone.integration.test.ts (3 tests, REAL Postgres, 84ms). Asserts the clone is kind='copy' with source_template_id=LIB, copyId != LIB; envelope columns copied (subject='Welcome', sender_id=SENDER, to_address='{{customer.email}}'); the send node's definition.template_id is repointed at the copy; editing the copy's subject to 'Edited copy' leaves the library subject 'Welcome' UNCHANGED; a cross-workspace template_id returns 404 with NO copy created (count before==after); a foreign campaign id returns 404. Handler attachCampaignSendTemplate uses scopedQuery(ctx.workspaceId) for the INSERT...SELECT source SELECT (workspace_id=$1 prepended), so a foreign template SELECTs no row -> empty RETURNING -> 404. workspace_id derived from ctx, never the body.

criteriaCoverage.criterion: Activating a campaign with a send node missing From/To/Subject is refused with a clear, node-specific message; once all are set + a verified domain exists, it activates. Verified against REAL Postgres.
criteriaCoverage.evidence: services/local-api/test/campaign-publish-gate.integration.test.ts (7 tests, REAL Postgres, 112ms). Ordered 409s: null sender_id -> /Choose who the email is from/i AND names the node ('send'); blank to_address -> /Set the To field/i; blank subject -> /Add a subject line/i. Fully-configured + verified domain -> 200, campaigns.status='active'. Verified-domain gate runs AFTER envelope checks: complete envelope but no verified domain -> 409 /verified sending domain/i. A no-send-node campaign (only set_attribute) activates ungated -> 200/active. Foreign campaign id -> 404 (inv.2). Gate uses collectSendNodeEnvelopeGaps (pure, BFS order) fed by a scopedQuery envelope read; verified-domain check is scopedQuery on sending_domains.

criteriaCoverage.criterion: A campaign send flows through outbox -> Dispatcher -> messages_log(campaign_id) with subject/To/body merge tags rendered; passes suppression/cap/quiet-hours. AWS/SES mocked.
criteriaCoverage.evidence: services/campaign-runner/test/campaign-send-dispatcher-instance.integration.test.ts (1 test, REAL Postgres + injected SES, 131ms). Runner emits ONE outbox row with campaign_id=CAMP and dedupe_key 'campaign:<camp>:<profile>:send'; the REAL dispatchOutbox then resolves From from sender_id ('"Acme Team" <team@mail.acme.com>', NO no-reply fallback), renders To ('gold@example.com' from {{customer.email}}), SUBJECT merge tags rendered ('Hi gold@example.com (gold)' — the v0.27.2 fix), body merge rendered ('Hello gold'); messages_log row has campaign_id=CAMP, broadcast_id NULL, ses_message_id set. EXACTLY-ONCE: replaying dispatchOutbox sends nothing more (ses.sends stays 1, one messages_log row). Dispatcher + packages/email are byte-for-byte UNCHANGED since the phase-3 commit (git diff 8710ad4 -- services/dispatcher packages/email == 0 lines). Existing action-send-through-dispatcher.integration.test.ts (2 tests) still covers suppression/unverified gating.

criteriaCoverage.criterion: An update-profile (set_attribute) step writes a LITERAL value AND an event-sourced value ({{event.amount}} from enrollment.state); workspace-scoped + idempotent on retry; undefined path safe; unit tests for the resolver.
criteriaCoverage.evidence: Unit: packages/shared/test/expression-value.test.ts (20 tests) — numeric literal preserved (42, not stringified), explicit null honored, legacy bare scalar = implicit literal, {{customer.tier}} shorthand='gold', {{customer.email}}=column, {{event.amount}}='19.99', deep {{event.items.0.sku}}='ABC-1', {{event.missing}}/'{{customer.unknownAttr}}'/no-event-ctx/{{order.total}} all safe-empty '' (never the raw token, never throws), surrounding text interpolated. services/campaign-runner/test/set-attribute-value-spec.test.ts (10) — pure validateSetAttributeValue (literal needs value, expression needs non-empty string, unknown kind rejected, bare scalar accepted) + side-effect carries the spec. enrollment-event-state.test.ts (5) — buildEnrollmentInsertWithState binds workspace_id $1, throws on falsy ws, persists {event:{type,payload,event_id}}, intent threads event only on the event path. Integration: set-attribute-event-sourced.integration.test.ts (2, REAL Postgres) — state.event.payload persisted, attributes.tier='gold' (literal), last_purchase_amount='19.99' (event-sourced, NOT the token), safe_missing='' (undefined path), other workspace's identically-emailed profile UNTOUCHED (workspace-scoped UPDATE), idempotent retry yields identical attrs, and a manual/segment enrollment (no state.event) resolves event.* safe-empty. SQL-INJECTION SAFETY (inv.6) CONFIRMED: resolveValueSpec is read-only string substitution over a merge map; the resolved value is then bound as a parameter into the existing buildSetAttribute (UPDATE profiles SET attributes=jsonb_set(...,$3::text[],$4::jsonb,true) WHERE workspace_id=$1 AND id=$2) — value goes into $4::jsonb stringified, key into $3::text[]; NEVER interpolated into SQL text.

recommendations: Optional: attachCampaignSendTemplate does not call validateSenderId on the clone, which is safe today because the clone only copies an already-validated sender_id from a workspace-scoped source template. If a future endpoint lets a caller set a send-node copy's sender_id directly, route it through validateSenderId like createTemplate/updateTemplate to keep inv.2 mechanical.

Minor cleanup (not blocking): packages/shared/src/event.ts exports resolveEventPath and expandEventToken that resolveValueSpec does not call directly (it relies on eventMerge's pre-flattened map). They are unit-tested and harmless symmetry with customer.ts; consider noting they are for symmetry/future use or trimming if unused.

When the builder UI lands (phases 5/6), wire the activate/attach endpoints to spinner-locked kit Buttons and surface the node-named 409 message via showToast; add Playwright coverage of the publish-gate flow in a real browser (out of scope this backend phase).
